title: lycianthes fugax
intro: Lycianthes fugax, also known as the purple flower or purple passion plant, is a flowering plant species native to South America, belonging to the Solanaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Lycianthes fugax include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Lycianthes fugax can be found in the form of extracts, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
